display.purpose: Hero titles and page-level identifiers. One instance per screen maximum. Always uppercase.

headline.purpose: Section headings. Uppercase. Continues the display font at section scale.

title.purpose: Card headings and post titles. Uppercase. Heaviest level at card scale.

body.purpose: Prose and descriptions. System font stack only — no web font loaded. Handles bilingual content natively.

label.purpose: All metadata: dates, tags, nav links, breadcrumbs, kickers, stat labels. Monospace uppercase — the operational readout voice.

shadows.name: hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Universal hover pattern. Nav links, post cards, buttons. Uses currentColor so elements inherit their shadow from text color.

shadows.name: accent-yellow
shadows.purpose: Work cards and stat boxes assigned to Writing/Yellow content category.

shadows.name: accent-cyan
shadows.purpose: Work cards and stat boxes assigned to Frontend/Cyan content category.

shadows.name: accent-violet
shadows.purpose: Work cards and stat boxes assigned to AI/Violet content category.

shadows.name: neutral-offset
shadows.purpose: Default surface elevation (--shadow-md). Editorial cards and panels. Nearly invisible — tonal separator only.

shadows.name: dossier-glow
shadows.purpose: Dossier sub-theme panels only. Phosphor green at 10% opacity — atmospheric, not structural.

motion.name: hover-lift
motion.purpose: Universal card and button hover transition. Fast enough to feel responsive, slow enough to be perceived.

motion.name: state-change
motion.purpose: Color state transitions — nav active state, filter button active state.

motion.name: dossier-scan
motion.purpose: Phosphor scan bar on the dossier photo. Atmospheric only — no UI meaning.

motion.name: dossier-blink
motion.purpose: Blinking cursor and classification dot in the dossier sub-theme.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary call-to-action. Wanted Yellow fill, hard corners, uppercase monospace label. Used for main CTAs and active navigation.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 0.85rem 1.05rem; border: 1px solid #333333; border-radius: 0; background: #ffd43b; color: #111111; font-family: "SFMono-Regular", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.84rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 120ms ease, box-shadow 120ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover, .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { transform: translate(-2px, -2px); box-shadow: 6px 6px 0 0 currentColor; outline: none; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translate(0, 0); box-shadow: none; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Secondary action. Dark panel background, aged-ivory text. Same shape and hover behavior as primary.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 0.85rem 1.05rem; border: 1px solid #333333; border-radius: 0; background: #1d1d1d; color: #f6ead6; font-family: "SFMono-Regular", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.84rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 120ms ease, box-shadow 120ms ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover, .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { transform: translate(-2px, -2px); box-shadow: 6px 6px 0 0 currentColor; outline: none; }

components.name: Navigation Links
components.description: Monospace uppercase links rendered as small bordered buttons. Active state: full Wanted Yellow fill.
components.html: WRITINGLABABOUT
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.65rem; } .ds-nav-link { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 0.5rem 0.8rem; border: 1px solid #333333; border-radius: 0; background: #111111; color: #f6ead6; font-family: "SFMono-Regular", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; text-decoration: none; box-shadow: 4px 4px 0 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.04); transition: transform 120ms ease, box-shadow 120ms ease; } .ds-nav-link:hover, .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { transform: translate(-2px, -2px); box-shadow: 6px 6px 0 0 currentColor; outline: none; } .ds-nav-link--active { background: #ffd43b; color: #111111; }

components.name: Work Card — Yellow
components.description: Primary content card for Writing/Tech content. Persistent Wanted Yellow offset shadow. On hover: lifts further.
components.html:
TECH WRITINGA-001

ENGINEERING NOTES

Backend systems, AI agents, and deployment practice.

12 articles→
components.css: .ds-work-card { display: grid; gap: 1rem; align-content: start; min-height: 18rem; padding: 1.35rem; border: 1px solid #333333; border-radius: 0; background: #111111; color: #f6ead6; text-decoration: none; transition: transform 120ms ease, box-shadow 120ms ease; } .ds-work-card--yellow { box-shadow: 6px 6px 0 0 #ffd43b; } .ds-work-card--cyan { box-shadow: 6px 6px 0 0 #00d4ff; } .ds-work-card--violet { box-shadow: 6px 6px 0 0 #b678ff; } .ds-work-card:hover, .ds-work-card:focus-visible { transform: translate(-2px, -2px); outline: none; } .ds-work-card--yellow:hover { box-shadow: 8px 8px 0 0 #ffd43b; } .ds-work-card--cyan:hover { box-shadow: 8px 8px 0 0 #00d4ff; } .ds-work-card--violet:hover { box-shadow: 8px 8px 0 0 #b678ff; } .ds-work-eyebrow { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; } .ds-work-kicker { font-family: "SFMono-Regular", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #9c9384; } .ds-work-title { margin: 0; font-family: "Arial Black", "Archivo Black", Impact, sans-serif; font-size: clamp(1.2rem, 2vw, 1.7rem); line-height: 1; letter-spacing: 0.02em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #f6ead6; } .ds-work-copy { margin: 0; color: #9c9384; font-size: 0.95rem; line-height: 1.7; } .ds-work-footer { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; margin-top: auto; padding-top: 0.75rem; border-top: 1px solid #333333; } .ds-work-meta { font-family: "SFMono-Regular", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #9c9384; }

components.name: Badge / Panel Kicker
components.description: Classification marker at label scale. Signal color at 10% opacity background, offset shadow. One color variant per content category.
components.html: FEATUREDFRONTENDLIVEAI
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.4rem; width: fit-content; padding: 0.35rem 0.55rem; border: 1px solid #333333; border-radius: 0; background: rgba(255,212,59,0.12); color: #f6ead6; font-family: "SFMono-Regular", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; box-shadow: 3px 3px 0 0 #333333; } .ds-badge--cyan { background: rgba(0,212,255,0.1); } .ds-badge--green { background: rgba(0,210,106,0.1); } .ds-badge--violet { background: rgba(182,120,255,0.1); } .ds-badge--red { background: rgba(255,77,77,0.1); }

components.name: Terminal Window
components.description: Signature component. CRT-style dark terminal with square traffic-light stops. Used for live system demonstrations.
components.html:
luke@classified:~

$ whoami

luke — senior engineer, self-styled AI model

$ status

● WANTED IN 47 JURISDICTIONS
components.css: .ds-terminal { border: 1px solid #333333; border-radius: 0; background: #0c0c0c; } .ds-terminal-bar { display: flex; gap: 0.35rem; align-items: center; padding: 0.7rem 0.8rem; border-bottom: 1px solid #333333; background: #151515; } .ds-terminal-bar span { width: 0.75rem; height: 0.75rem; border-radius: 0; background: #9c9384; flex: 0 0 auto; } .ds-terminal-bar span:nth-child(1) { background: #ff4d4d; } .ds-terminal-bar span:nth-child(2) { background: #ffd43b; } .ds-terminal-bar span:nth-child(3) { background: #00d26a; } .ds-terminal-title { margin-left: 0.5rem; font-family: "SFMono-Regular", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; color: #9c9384; letter-spacing: 0.04em; background: none; width: auto; height: auto; border-radius: 0; } .ds-terminal-body { padding: 1.2rem; font-family: "SFMono-Regular", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.86rem; line-height: 1.8; color: #d6f7df; } .ds-terminal-body p { margin: 0; }

components.name: Post Card
components.description: Editorial content card for blog posts. Neutral offset shadow at rest; currentColor lift on hover.
components.html:
BACKEND

BUILDING DISTRIBUTED SYSTEMS

A practical guide to consistency, fault tolerance, and the lies we tell ourselves about CAP theorem.

2026-07-14→
components.css: .ds-post-card { display: grid; gap: 0.9rem; align-content: start; padding: 1.35rem; border: 1px solid #333333; border-radius: 0; background: #111111; color: #f6ead6; text-decoration: none; box-shadow: 8px 8px 0 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.05); transition: transform 120ms ease, box-shadow 120ms ease; } .ds-post-card:hover, .ds-post-card:focus-visible { transform: translate(-2px, -2px); box-shadow: 6px 6px 0 0 currentColor; outline: none; } .ds-post-tag { font-family: "SFMono-Regular", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.74rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #ffd43b; } .ds-post-title { margin: 0; font-family: "Arial Black", "Archivo Black", Impact, sans-serif; font-size: 1.2rem; line-height: 1.05; text-transform: uppercase; color: #f6ead6; } .ds-post-copy { margin: 0; color: #9c9384; line-height: 1.7; font-size: 0.95rem; } .ds-post-meta { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; padding-top: 0.75rem; border-top: 1px solid #333333; } .ds-post-date { font-family: "SFMono-Regular", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 700; color: #9c9384; }

components.name: Stat Box
components.description: Compact metric display. Display font for the number, mono label for context. Color variant via accent offset shadow.
components.css: .ds-stat-box { min-width: 9rem; padding: 0.85rem 0.95rem; border: 1px solid #333333; border-radius: 0; background: #1d1d1d; } .ds-stat-box--yellow { box-shadow: 6px 6px 0 0 #ffd43b; } .ds-stat-box--cyan { box-shadow: 6px 6px 0 0 #00d4ff; } .ds-stat-box--violet { box-shadow: 6px 6px 0 0 #b678ff; } .ds-stat-label { display: block; margin-bottom: 0.35rem; font-family: "SFMono-Regular", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #9c9384; } .ds-stat-number { font-family: "Arial Black", "Archivo Black", Impact, sans-serif; font-size: 1.35rem; line-height: 1; color: #f6ead6; }

narrative.overview:
Luke Lab is a classified intelligence file on a self-styled AI model — the most wanted entity in a hacker's cyberpunk world. The interface is not a portfolio. It is a dossier: dark terminals, hardwired transmissions, redacted fields, colored offset shadows that read like physical stamps. The premise is taken seriously enough to make it feel real.

The visual language is cyberpunk-retro: carbon black backgrounds, aged-ivory text that feels like a transmission received on a CRT, and high-saturation accent colors deployed with military discipline — each one assigned a signal role in the information hierarchy, like category markers on a mission board.

This system is dark because the scenario demands it: an operator's dossier, read under operational conditions. Not dark because tools look cool dark. Dark because the narrative requires it.
keyCharacteristics: Zero border-radius throughout (pill tags at 999px are the sole exception)
Hard offset box-shadow as the only elevation mechanism: colored, crisp, zero blur
Monospace uppercase labels everywhere metadata appears
Display type compressed to line-height 0.88–0.95, always uppercase
Five named accent colors, each assigned to one content category
Faint dot-grid background at the body level — a data-space underlay
System font stack for body/label: no web font loaded, bilingual-safe

rules.name: The One Channel Rule
rules.body: Each accent color is the sole identifier for its content category: Yellow = Writing, Cyan = Frontend, Green = Deploy/Live, Violet = AI, Red = Alert. Do not reassign. Do not mix channels on a single surface. The color is the category. Its scarcity is its meaning.

rules.name: The Carbon Floor Rule
rules.body: The background is always Carbon Black (#171717). Never #000000 — pure black feels valueless. Never dark blue or dark teal — the domain reflex is the enemy. The floor must not have a recognizable genre.

rules.name: The Uppercase Display Rule
rules.body: Any text using the display font (Arial Black / Archivo Black) is uppercase. Sentence-case in the display font is a mistake, not a choice.

rules.name: The Body Stack Rule
rules.body: Body text must never load a remote font. The system stack renders instantly across platforms and handles bilingual content correctly without Chinese-character fallback artifacts.

rules.name: The Mono Label Rule
rules.body: Any text smaller than 0.85rem carrying metadata is monospace and uppercase. Lowercase proportional type at label scale signals a decision that was not made.

rules.name: The No-Blur Rule
rules.body: A box-shadow with non-zero blur radius in a UI elevation role is prohibited. If it looks like a soft shadow, it is wrong. The only permitted blur is filter: blur() on purely decorative atmospheric pseudo-elements — never on interactive surfaces.

rules.name: The Flat-at-Rest Rule
rules.body: No surface has a persistent elevation shadow in its default rest state, except work cards and stat boxes with named accent offsets. Everything else is flat until interaction.

dos: Do use box-shadow: Npx Npx 0 [color] (zero blur) for every elevation effect — hard offset only.
Do assign accent colors by content category and hold the assignment: Yellow = Writing, Cyan = Frontend, Green = Deploy, Violet = AI, Red = Alert.
Do use monospace uppercase for every label, date, kicker, breadcrumb, and nav link without exception.
Do compress display headings to line-height: 0.88–0.95 and uppercase. The stamp-like compression is the identity.
Do apply translate(-2px, -2px) + shadow expansion at 120ms ease as the universal hover pattern.
Do maintain the body-level grid background (rgba(255,255,255,0.035) lines at 24px). It grounds the surface in a data-space register.
Do keep total accent coverage under 10% per screen. Carbon and iron dominate — color is signal, not decoration.
Do use 999px border-radius exclusively for pill tags. The contrast against zero-radius surfaces makes pills legible as a different affordance.
donts: Don't use rounded corners (2px, 4px, 8px, rounded-lg) anywhere except 999px pills. Zero-radius is non-negotiable.
Don't use glassmorphism (backdrop-filter: blur on card or overlay surfaces). Explicitly rejected.
Don't use soft blurred drop shadows. A box-shadow with non-zero blur in any UI elevation role is wrong.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text with a gradient fill). All text is a single solid color.
Don't build hero-metric templates: big number, supporting stats, gradient accent. Commercial SaaS cliché.
Don't use #000000 as any background. The floor is Carbon Black (#171717).
Don't mix multiple accent colors on a single surface. One module, one channel.
Don't load a web font for body or label text. The system stack handles bilingual content correctly and loads instantly.
Don't use Dossier sub-theme components (scan panels, registration marks, blinking cursors, redacted fields) outside the dedicated dossier/about section.

Don't make the design feel dark through genre reflex — dark blue + electric neon reads as crypto; dark green reads as finance terminal. Carbon Black with warm ivory is a deliberate departure from those domain reflexes.
